Title: Abscisic acid regulates secondary cell-wall formation and lignin deposition in Arabidopsis thaliana through phosphorylation of NST1
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased expression of SCW-related genes but did not significantly alter stem SCW deposition or lignification pattern
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression; microscopy; RT-qPCR of 13 SCW-related genes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCED6 overexpression was used to elevate ABA biosynthetic capacity. 2. SCW-related transcription factors and biosynthetic genes were upregulated. 3. No clear increase in lignified SCW phenotype was observed in stems.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical support, water transport, stress adaptation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential negative feedback or hormonal cross-talk may buffer overlignification
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because overexpression lines were experimentally analyzed in Results, but NCED6 was not a main mechanistic focus compared with ABA2, SnRK2s, and NST1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Increasing ABA biosynthesis alone may not be sufficient to enhance lignification/SCW, suggesting limited standalone engineering value

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased SCW-related gene expression without detectable alteration of stem lignification or SCW morphology
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCED9 overexpression elevated transcription of SCW-related genes. 2. Stem SCW and lignification were not significantly changed morphologically. 3. Results support transcriptional but not sufficient morphological control by increased ABA biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical support, water transport, stress adaptation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible feedback regulation limits excessive lignification
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Experimentally tested but secondary to the main NST1-SnRK2 mechanism
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggests ABA biosynthesis enhancement alone may not robustly improve wall deposition traits

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly accumulated in the second internode of inflorescence stems rich in vascular tissues undergoing SCW thickening
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key ABA biosynthetic step; not explicitly stated as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Inflorescence stem vascular tissues/fibers and vessels for downstream lignified SCW phenotype
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for ABA, which positively promotes SCW deposition and lignin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native endogenous promoter in mutant background
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene expression interrogation using Arabidopsis eFP Browser; meta-analysis of public microarray datasets via Genevestigator; accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts xanthoxin to abscisic aldehyde in ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Xanthoxin dehydrogenase / short-chain alcohol dehydrogenase ABA2
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Endogenous ABA deficiency; stem developmental stage
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutant analysis; microscopy; lignin/cellulose quantification; RT-qPCR of SCW genes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of ABA2 reduces endogenous ABA and leads to thinner secondary cell walls, reduced lignification, reduced cellulose, and lower SCW-related transcript levels
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical support, water and nutrient conduction, stress resistance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating ABA2 may alter lignin/SCW deposition, but reduced ABA biosynthesis can weaken stems and likely impair stress adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although ABA2 is in ABA biosynthesis rather than lignin biosynthesis, it is experimentally central because its mutation alters SCW and lignin phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABA2 loss-of-function reduced SCW thickness in vessels and fibers. 2. aba2-1 stems showed reduced lignin and crystalline cellulose contents. 3. SCW-related transcription factors and biosynthetic genes were broadly downregulated in aba2-1.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Active in inflorescence stem fiber cells undergoing SCW thickening; transcript reduced in snrk2.2/3/6 mutant relative to wild type
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; in vitro pull-down; BiFC; in vitro phosphorylation assay; transgenic complementation in nst1/snd1; dual-luciferase transactivation; ABA-response expression assays
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Inflorescence stem interfascicular fibers, xylary fibers, and vessels
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: S316A phospho-defective form fails to restore normal stem strength and fiber SCW deposition in nst1/snd1 background
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator and phosphorylation-dependent activator of SCW and lignin deposition in fiber cells
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of secondary cell wall biosynthesis including lignin, cellulose, and xylan deposition
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA signaling through SnRK2.2, SnRK2.3, and SnRK2.6; osmotic stress mentioned as possible SnRK2 activator
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Physically interacts with SnRK2.2; SnRK2.3; SnRK2.6
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NST1 physically interacts with SnRK2.2/2.3/2.6 in the nucleus. 2. SnRK2s phosphorylate NST1 primarily at Ser316. 3. Ser316 is required for full transactivation of MYB46, MYB83, and NST1 promoters and for restoration of SCW/lignin phenotypes. 4. ABA-induced MYB46 and PAL4 expression depends partly on phosphorylatable NST1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Phosphorylated NST1 promotes SCW thickening and lignin deposition; the S316A mutant shows strongly impaired function
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Ser316 phosphorylation site is conserved in dicot NST1 orthologs but absent from monocot NST1s and from Arabidopsis SND1/NST3
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates MYB46, MYB83, NST1 autoregulation, and ABA-inducible PAL4 expression
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive autoregulation of NST1 promoter; likely feedforward activation through MYB46/MYB83
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SnRK2.2; SnRK2.3; SnRK2.6; MYB46 promoter; MYB83 promoter; NST1 promoter
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: PhosPhAt phosphosite database retrieval for NST1 phosphopeptide; Genevestigator microarray meta-analysis for SCW-related genes; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native NST1 promoter in complementation lines; MYB46, MYB83, and NST1 promoters in dual luciferase assays
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to modulate lignin/SCW in dicots via phospho-site engineering or ABA-responsive tuning, but altered NST1 activity can affect stem strength and wall properties
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment (10 nM, 4 h); developmental SCW formation in stems
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical support, water transport, stress adaptation

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly accumulated in the second internode of inflorescence stems
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Triple mutant phenotyping with snrk2.2/snrk2.3/snrk2.6; Y2H; pull-down; BiFC; in vitro kinase assay; protoplast transactivation context
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Inflorescence stem fiber and vessel secondary walls
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes severe SCW thinning, lower lignin/cellulose, and ABA insensitivity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core ABA-signaling kinase required for full ABA-dependent activation of NST1 and normal lignification
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Posttranslational activation of SCW transcriptional machinery
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA core signaling; relief from PP2C inhibition; osmotic stress can also activate SnRK2s
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. snrk2.2/3/6 triple mutant showed the strongest SCW and lignin defects. 2. SnRK2.2 physically interacts with NST1 in nucleus and in vitro. 3. SnRK2.2 phosphorylates NST1 at Ser316 in vitro. 4. SnRK2 function is required for efficient NST1 transactivation and ABA-induced downstream gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignification and SCW deposition through phosphorylation-dependent activation of NST1
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function inferred partly from triple mutant rather than single-gene knockout in this study
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly activates MYB46, MYB83, NST1, PAL4 via NST1 phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Arabidopsis eFP Browser; Genevestigator public microarray meta-analysis; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential node to tune ABA-responsive wall deposition, but broad ABA roles may cause pleiotropic effects
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment; developmental stem SCW formation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical support, water transport, stress adaptation

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in second internode, less highly accumulated than SnRK2.2 and SnRK2.6
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Triple mutant phenotyping; Y2H; pull-down; BiFC; in vitro kinase assay
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Inflorescence stem fiber and vessel secondary walls
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of triple mutant causing severe SCW and lignin defects
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes with SnRK2.2/2.6 to ABA-dependent phosphorylation of NST1
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Posttranslational control of SCW transcriptional activator
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnRK2.3 is part of the core kinase module required for normal SCW deposition. 2. SnRK2.3 physically interacts with NST1. 3. SnRK2.3 phosphorylates NST1 in vitro. 4. Its role was shown in combination with SnRK2.2 and SnRK2.6.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports ABA-dependent lignification and SCW thickening through NST1 phosphorylation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Individual contribution not separated from triple mutant in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Stem/vascular expression lower than SnRK2.2 and SnRK2.6
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Arabidopsis eFP Browser; Genevestigator public microarray meta-analysis; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of ABA-responsive engineering but likely pleiotropic because of broad stress signaling functions
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment; developmental stem SCW formation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical support, water transport, stress adaptation

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly accumulated in the second internode of inflorescence stems
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Triple mutant phenotyping; Y2H library screen and directed test; pull-down; BiFC; in vitro kinase assay
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Inflorescence stem fiber and vessel secondary walls
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss with SnRK2.2/2.3 causes severe reduction in lignin and cellulose and thinner walls
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central ABA-signaling kinase that identified NST1 as interactor and phosphorylates it to promote SCW/lignin deposition
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Posttranslational control of SCW transcriptional activator
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ABA signaling via PP2C/PYL module; osmotic stress
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnRK2.6 was used as bait to identify NST1 in Y2H screening. 2. It physically interacts with NST1 in vitro and in vivo. 3. It phosphorylates NST1 mainly at Ser316 in vitro. 4. Together with SnRK2.2/2.3 it is required for ABA induction of downstream SCW genes.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignification and secondary wall deposition through ABA-responsive phosphorylation of NST1
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also known as OST1/SRK2E; role supported mainly in the SnRK2.2/3/6 module
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Arabidopsis eFP Browser; Genevestigator public microarray meta-analysis; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target for modulating wall deposition under stress, but broad ABA signaling roles require caution
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ABA treatment; developmental stem SCW formation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mechanical support, water transport, stress adaptation
